Commercial Mar 26, 2026

8 Hours In A Boeing 737 MAX? Inside United Airlines' New Longest Nonstop Flights

Half of the ten longest routes started in 2025 or will do so in 2026. See them all here!

United's expansion of ultra-long 737 MAX routes signals airlines' confidence in the aircraft's reliability and economics for transcontinental markets after its safety crisis.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

I'm A Flight Attendant, And These Are The Things I Wish Every Passenger Knew Before Boarding

Practical advice for passengers that makes flights easier.

Flight attendants prioritize safety procedures and emergency protocols over passenger comfort requests, making pre-flight briefing attention critical for collective well-being.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

New Route & 9 More Boosted: JetBlue Keeps Turning Up The Heat In Fort Lauderdale

JetBlue adds Cleveland and boosts nine routes as Fort Lauderdale growth accelerates again.

JetBlue's expansion of 10 routes from Fort Lauderdale signals competitive pressure from low-cost carriers forcing legacy carriers to increase frequency on secondary markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Aircraft Set To Replace Delta Air Lines' Iconic Boeing 717

Delta's 717 fleet is soon to be retired, and the airline will be replacing the type's operations with the Airbus A220 family.

Delta's A220 transition will reduce its narrow-body fleet complexity while gaining superior fuel efficiency and lower operating costs per seat on regional routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The 10 Most Popular Transpacific Routes Operated By The Boeing 787 Dreamliner In 2026

The 787 and Transpacific routes really go hand-in-hand. The aircraft provides the capacity and efficiency to make these routes work.

The 787's fuel efficiency and 242-330 seat capacity enable profitable operations on thinner transpacific routes that would be unviable for larger widebodies.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why selected controls are appropriate and justify acceptance of remaining risks rather than defaulting to maximum mitigation.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April 2026 offers optimal viewing conditions for celestial events that can inform aviation weather planning and crew situational awareness during night operations.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope completes construction and enters final pre-launch testing phase, positioning for deployment that will advance astronomical observation capabilities.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Simple Flying Podcast Episode 284: Air Canada Crash Latest, United Airlines Introduces Couches In Economy Class

Join Tom & Channing for the latest aviation hot topics.

Major carriers are simultaneously addressing safety incidents and pursuing unconventional cabin innovations, signaling industry priorities shifting toward both operational reliability and competitive differentiation.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman's completion marks NASA's next major infrared observatory ready for launch, positioning the agency to advance exoplanet detection and dark energy research beyond Hubble's capabilities.

See NASA →

PAI
Safety Mar 26, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Strong safety reporting cultures reduce incident detection lag, meaning organizations that psychologically enable crew honesty identify hazards weeks rather than months after occurrence.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

Artemis II's international crew composition, including Canada's first lunar mission astronaut, signals NASA's shift toward multinational deep-space exploration partnerships.

See NASA →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Booking Last-Minute Business Class Award Tickets: Everything You Need To Know

Last-minute business class award tickets can offer significant savings relative to purchasing the fares in cash, although this is not guaranteed.

Award ticket availability for business class peaks within 24-48 hours of departure as airlines release inventory, creating arbitrage opportunities for savvy frequent flyers despite unpredictable pricing dynamics.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Navi AI launches publicly with $6 million to expand AI pilot training platform

Navi AI has launched publicly with more than $6 million in funding as it looks to expand its…...

AI-powered pilot training platforms are attracting significant venture funding, signaling investor confidence in digital transformation of aviation's historically conservative training ecosystem.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Specialized thermal management expertise is critical for preserving scientific integrity during ISS cargo missions, requiring close coordination between ground systems and payload developers.

See NASA →

▶
Safety Mar 26, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design at major hubs can create confusion during high-traffic periods, requiring pilots to exercise heightened situational awareness during post-landing procedures.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Airbus Tests Multi-Aircraft Aerial Firefighting System

Trial linked aircraft, drones and ground crews for enhanced targeting and coordination.

Integrated aerial firefighting networks combining manned aircraft, drones, and ground coordination could significantly improve wildfire suppression efficiency and crew safety through real-time data sharing.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Russian YouTuber killed after airplane may have been mistaken for Ukraine drone

Russian aviation blogger Pavel Koshkin was killed when the Alto NG aircraft he was flying crashed near Kolomna,…...

Civilian aircraft misidentification risks are escalating in active conflict zones, creating deadly hazards for general aviation operators beyond combat areas.

See AeroTime Hub →

General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 26, 2026

Departing Geneva toward the Matterhorn.

Alpine terrain photography demonstrates the visual appeal aviation maintains for marketing while highlighting operational challenges of high-altitude proximity flying.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

“Please Do Something”: Pilots Have Complained About LaGuardia Airport’s Safety For Years

Pilots at LaGuardia Airport have reported multiple near-miss situations due to unclear instructions from air traffic control.

Systemic ATC communication failures at LaGuardia have generated sustained pilot complaints, indicating regulatory oversight gaps requiring immediate operational protocol review.

See Simple Flying →

▶
Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities remain preventable through enhanced situational awareness and terrain planning, yet pilot overconfidence in reaction time remains the primary human factor barrier.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

House Committee Advances ALERT Act

Measure includes collision-mitigation requirements, changes for military aircraft operations and limits on some ADS-B data use.

Lawmakers are balancing safety modernization with military operational flexibility and privacy concerns by restricting ADS-B transparency while mandating collision avoidance technology.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

This Airline Has The World’s Most Comfortable Premium Economy Seat In 2026

The Dubai-based carrier's premium economy product is relatively new.

Emirates' new premium economy seat design sets a competitive benchmark that could pressure rivals to upgrade their mid-tier cabin products to avoid losing premium-conscious passengers.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find

For the second consecutive year, winter sea ice in the Arctic reached a level that matches the lowest peak observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979. On March 15, Arctic sea ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles (14.29 million square kilometers), very close to the 2025 peak of 5.53...

Declining Arctic sea ice directly impacts aviation operations through altered weather patterns, atmospheric circulation changes, and increased routing complexity for polar overflights.

See NASA →

Military Mar 26, 2026

5 Structural Advantages Of The A-10 Warthog For Close Air Support Missions

This aircraft offers some impressive offensive and defensive capabilities.

The A-10's redundant systems and armor placement prioritize pilot survivability in high-threat environments, making structural resilience as critical as firepower for CAS effectiveness.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers—not just aircraft technology—is critical for meeting sustainability goals as traffic volumes continue climbing.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy...

IXPE's polarimetry data reveals magnetic field structures in supernova remnants, providing insights applicable to understanding cosmic particle acceleration mechanisms relevant to space weather forecasting.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Colorado’s Rocky Mountain Metro Airport Launches Unleaded Avgas

UL94 sales begin with state-backed price support and STC reimbursement.

State-level pricing subsidies and certification reimbursement could accelerate unleaded fuel adoption by removing primary economic barriers for operators and FBOs.

See AVweb →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic fighter assembly capability strengthens Latin American military independence while expanding Saab's production footprint beyond Nordic operations.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Dreamliner groundings push Kenya Airways back into the red

Kenya Airways returned to financial losses in 2025, the airline reported on March 24, 2026. The Kenyan flag…...

Aircraft groundings demonstrate how supply chain disruptions in widebody fleets can rapidly undermine profitability for carriers dependent on specific aircraft types.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

AMA Advocacy: H.R. 6460 passes U.S. House of Representatives

On Tuesday, March 24, H.R. 6460, a bill championed by AMA that will simplify altitude...

H.R. 6460's passage enables recreational drone operators to fly at higher altitudes without waiver requirements, reducing regulatory friction for hobbyists and potentially expanding commercial airspace access.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain

Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Friday, March 20, 2026 Curiosity has just concluded a very intense week of science observations and engineering activities, as it wraps up its monthslong investigation...

Curiosity's extended investigation of boxwork terrain demonstrates how rover mission planning balances intensive science collection with engineering constraints to maximize data value over multi-month operational periods.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

The FAA's records demand signals it may be preparing enforcement action or regulatory intervention beyond typical oversight, escalating a dispute that could affect how states fund airport infrastructure nationally.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's April 1 target date for Artemis II represents a critical validation flight for the SLS-Orion system before lunar landing missions resume.

See NASA →

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's 1-inch sensor and omnidirectional obstacle avoidance in consumer FPV drones indicate the technology gap between recreational and professional aerial imaging is rapidly narrowing.

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

Strategic weapons reallocation decisions between theaters signal shifting defense priorities that could affect aerospace supply chains and allied air defense capabilities globally.
